narrative: Dear Maria, imagine the admissions team looking at your application like they're piecing together the story of your journey to the bar exam, drawing from patterns they've seen in thousands of students just like you. Your Year 3 decile of 1 stood out as the biggest chapter pulling the narrative toward a tougher road ahead, signaling that even after a few years in law school, your grades placed you at the bottom of your class compared to peers who typically power through. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 1, painting an early picture of challenges in keeping up with the demanding pace right from the start. Your LSAT score of 31 added weight to that storyline, as it's on the lower side of what the model has seen succeed in high-stakes bar prep. Even with your upper-middle family income, which might offer some support, it didn't shift the tale enough in your favor here. Your Hispanic background entered the story too, reflecting hurdles that similar students have faced in the data. Your undergrad GPA of 3.1 continued that thread of steady but not standout performance, while choosing full-time study nudged things further toward the riskier path. The one bright note was your gender, which gently tugged the prediction toward success, like a subtle undercurrent of resilience seen in many women who've made it through. Together, these elements wove a picture where the model saw a 59.9% chance of not passing the bar, tipping the scales against your application. It's not the end of your story, though—many have rewritten similar scripts with targeted prep or different paths. What small steps could you take next to strengthen those early chapters? We're rooting for your next draft.
